talk_title: Inducing viral mimicry to turn cold small cell lung cancer hot via DHX9, TREX1 and spliceosome inhibition

speaker.name: Israel Cañadas
speaker.affiliation: Fox Chase Cancer Center, Assistant Professor

biomarkers.name: PD-L1
biomarkers.finding: PD-L1 expression induced after DHX9 or spliceosome inhibition in vitro

biomarkers.name: CD8 T cell infiltration
biomarkers.finding: Increased CD8+ T cells in tumors with DHX9 knockdown or spliceosome inhibitor treatment

models.name: immunocompetent small cell lung cancer model
models.genotype_or_key_features: DHX9 knockdown or TREX1 knockout, treated with anti-PD-1

models.name: human small cell lung cancer cell lines (e.g., HCT9-AR)

MOA_summary: Inhibition or genetic loss of DHX9, TREX1, or spliceosome components leads to accumulation of dsRNA, DNA‑RNA hybrids and crRNA, activating MDA5/MAVS and cGAS‑STING pathways, inducing type I interferon, cytokine production and necroptosis, which remodels the tumor microenvironment, increases CD8+ T‑cell infiltration and sensitizes small cell lung cancer to anti‑PD‑1 therapy.
key_findings: DHX9 knockdown in small cell lung cancer cells increases dsRNA and DNA‑RNA hybrids, activating STING and MDA5 pathways.
DHX9 loss reduces tumor growth and enhances CD8+ T‑cell infiltration in immunocompetent mice.
Combination of DHX9 knockdown with anti‑PD‑1 dramatically improves tumor control and survival versus anti‑PD‑1 alone.
Spliceosome inhibition (Pladienolide B) induces crRNA accumulation, ZBP1‑dependent necroptosis, and tumor regression.
Spliceosome inhibitor plus anti‑PD‑1 shows synergistic tumor suppression; effect is lost in CBP1‑deficient mice.
TREX1 is up‑regulated in chemotherapy‑resistant SCLC cells; its knockdown restores interferon signaling and sensitizes cells to cisplatin and T‑cell activation.
Viral mimicry can be chronic, leading to immune evasion; targeting TREX1 or DHX9 may overcome this tolerance.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Chronic viral mimicry can induce immune evasion via p53 loss and tolerance to cytosolic nucleases.
TREX1 up‑regulation in chemo‑resistant cells suppresses cGAS‑STING signaling, contributing to therapy resistance.

combination_strategies.rationale: Viral mimicry increases antigen presentation and PD‑L1 expression, making tumors more responsive to checkpoint blockade.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Synergistic tumor regression and prolonged survival in mouse SCLC models

combination_strategies.rationale: TREX1 knockdown restores cGAS‑STING activation and DNA damage response, sensitizing cells to DNA‑damaging agents.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Enhanced cisplatin efficacy in TREX1‑deficient SCLC xenografts

open_questions: What determines the balance between dsRNA and crRNA formation after spliceosome inhibition?
How does viral mimicry in tumor‑infiltrating lymphocytes affect autoimmunity or systemic immunity?
Can CBP1 expression levels predict response to spliceosome‑targeted therapies?

talk_title: Epigenetic regulation of viral mimicry and its impact on immunotherapy response

targets.alteration: wild-type vs R175H mutant vs null

biomarkers.name: type I interferon signature
biomarkers.finding: Induced by DNMTi and HDACi across multiple tumor models

biomarkers.name: PD‑L1 expression
biomarkers.finding: Upregulated after epigenetic therapy, correlating with improved checkpoint response

biomarkers.name: M2 macrophage abundance
biomarkers.finding: Reduced after combined DNMTi + ADAR1 knockdown in ovarian cancer model

models.name: DNMTi + anti‑PD‑1 in NKT cell lymphoma (phase II)
high‑grade serous ovarian cancer survivors
models.genotype_or_key_features: long‑term vs short‑term survivors, all TP53 mutant

clinical_data.value: significantly longer in high TE expression cluster (C1)
clinical_data.population: high‑grade serous ovarian cancer long‑term survivors

clinical_data.value: significantly longer in high TE expression cluster
improved when DNMTi combined with anti‑PD‑1 in melanoma preclinical model

MOA_summary: DNMT and HDAC inhibition demethylates and opens chromatin, reactivating transposable element transcription that forms dsRNA or DNA‑RNA hybrids, which are sensed by MDA5/MAVS and cGAS‑STING pathways, driving type I interferon signaling, upregulating antigen presentation and PD‑L1, and reshaping the tumor microenvironment to enhance checkpoint blockade efficacy; ADAR1 editing dampens this response, and TP53 status modulates TE expression and drug responsiveness.
readouts: IHC for PD‑L1 and immune infiltrates
key_findings: DNMT inhibitors reactivate endogenous retroviruses, generating dsRNA that triggers innate immunity.
Combining DNMTi with HDACi amplifies TE transcription and interferon responses.
ADAR1 knockdown synergizes with DNMTi to improve survival and shift macrophage polarization in ovarian cancer models.
Wild‑type TP53 binds to LTRs and enhances TE transcription upon DNMTi; TP53 loss reduces DNA methylation globally, increasing TE expression.
TP53‑null cells show the strongest interferon response to DNMTi, whereas R175H mutants are less responsive.
High TE expression in long‑term ovarian cancer survivors correlates with increased immune infiltration and better survival, independent of BRCA/HRD status.
Clinical trials of DNMTi + checkpoint blockade show mixed results; notable activity in NKT cell lymphoma and melanoma.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Chronic TE expression can lead to interferon desensitization and immune evasion.
ADAR1-mediated RNA editing reduces dsRNA immunogenicity, limiting viral mimicry efficacy.

combination_strategies.rationale: Viral mimicry upregulates antigen presentation and PD‑L1, sensitizing tumors to checkpoint blockade.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Improved tumor control and survival in preclinical melanoma and ovarian models

combination_strategies.rationale: HDAC inhibition synergizes with DNMTi to further increase TE transcription.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Higher interferon scores than DNMTi alone

combination_strategies.rationale: Blocks RNA editing that dampens dsRNA sensing, enhancing interferon response.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Extended survival and M1 macrophage polarization in ovarian cancer model

open_questions: How does TP53 status mechanistically influence DNMTi‑induced TE activation beyond methylation changes?
What are the determinants of chronic versus acute viral mimicry and their impact on therapy resistance?
Can TE expression be used prospectively to stratify patients for epigenetic‑immunotherapy combinations?

talk_title: Viral mimicry enhances cancer vaccine efficacy via interferon‑dependent tumor remodeling

targets.dependency_context: tumor cells for viral mimicry induction

biomarkers.name: type I interferon score
biomarkers.finding: Azacitidine induces strong IFN response in tumor cells, dependent on MAVS/STING

biomarkers.name: MHC class I expression
biomarkers.finding: Increased on tumor cells only when viral mimicry is intact

biomarkers.name: vaccine‑specific CD8+ T‑cell clones
biomarkers.finding: Clonal expansion in TME requires tumor‑intrinsic viral mimicry

models.name: B16‑F10 melanoma
models.genotype_or_key_features: wild‑type, MAVS‑/‑ or STING‑/‑ tumors; vaccinated with mRNA neoantigen

MOA_summary: Azacitidine demethylates tumor DNA, reactivating transposable elements that generate dsRNA and DNA‑RNA hybrids, activating MDA5/MAVS and cGAS‑STING pathways, leading to type I interferon production, upregulation of MHC‑I and PD‑L1, and remodeling of myeloid populations; this creates a permissive microenvironment for vaccine‑elicited T cells to infiltrate, expand, and mediate tumor control, an effect lost when MAVS or STING are knocked out in tumor cells.
key_findings: Azacitidine strongly induces transposable element transcription and a type I interferon response in B16 melanoma, dependent on MAVS and STING.
Vaccination alone fails therapeutically, but combined with azacitidine leads to tumor control and improved survival across multiple tumor models.
The combination increases tumor cell MHC‑I expression and expands vaccine‑specific CD8+ T‑cell clones within the TME.
MAVS/STING knockout tumors do not respond to the combination, confirming the necessity of tumor‑intrinsic viral mimicry.
Single‑cell analysis reveals increased cDC1 dendritic cells and reduced immunosuppressive Arg1+ macrophages only when viral mimicry is intact.
Combination therapy promotes proliferation of CD8+ T cells and clonal expansion of vaccine‑specific TCRs.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Tumors lacking MAVS or STING are resistant to the vaccine‑azacitidine synergy, highlighting dependence on innate sensing pathways.

combination_strategies.rationale: Viral mimicry upregulates antigen presentation and creates an inflamed TME, enabling vaccine‑elicited T cells to function.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Synergistic tumor regression and survival benefit in multiple syngeneic models

combination_strategies.rationale: Azacitidine‑induced PD‑L1 upregulation may sensitize tumors to PD‑1 inhibition.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Improved responses in NKT cell lymphoma patients (clinical observation)

open_questions: Can STING agonists substitute for azacitidine to achieve similar vaccine synergy?
What is the durability of myeloid repolarization after transient azacitidine treatment?
Will this strategy translate to human tumors with heterogeneous MAVS/STING status?
